body:
### Problem

I encountered the following failure when I entered the `:s` command, then pasted different content than intended.
```
:%s/x/y/222222222nvim: .../neovim/src/nvim/charset.c:1134: getdigits_int: Assertion `number >= INT_MIN && number <= INT_MAX' failed.
                                                                                                                                                          {134}
```

```cpp
#0  __pthread_kill_implementation (threadid=<optimized out>, signo=signo@entry=6, no_tid=no_tid@entry=0) at ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:44
#1  0x00007f74f7f5fdbf in __pthread_kill_internal (threadid=<optimized out>, signo=6) at ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:89
#2  0x00007f74f7f08d02 in __GI_raise (sig=sig@entry=6) at ../sysdeps/posix/raise.c:26
#3  0x00007f74f7ef04b2 in __GI_abort () at ./stdlib/abort.c:77
#4  0x00007f74f7ef0424 in __assert_fail_base (fmt=<optimized out>, assertion=<optimized out>, file=<optimized out>, line=<optimized out>, function=0x556533d9d0b0 <__PRETTY_FUNCTION__.1.lto_priv.10> "getdigits_int") at ./assert/assert.c:118
#5  0x00007f74f7f0144c in __assert_fail (assertion=<optimized out>, file=<optimized out>, line=<optimized out>, function=<optimized out>) at ./assert/assert.c:127
#6  0x00005565339227c4 in getdigits_int (pp=<optimized out>, strict=<optimized out>, def=<optimized out>) at .../src/nvim/charset.c:1134
#7  getdigits_int (pp=0x7ffc5aea5738, strict=true, def=2147483647) at .../src/nvim/charset.c:1129
#8  do_sub (eap=eap@entry=0x7ffc5aea6090, timeout=15365772818832, cmdpreview_ns=3, cmdpreview_bufnr=0) at .../src/nvim/ex_cmds.c:3674
#9  0x0000556533a40ee0 in ex_substitute_preview (eap=0x7ffc5aea6090, cmdpreview_ns=<optimized out>, cmdpreview_bufnr=<optimized out>) at .../src/nvim/ex_cmds.c:4977
#10 0x0000556533a42f42 in execute_cmd0 (retv=retv@entry=0x7ffc5aea5934, eap=eap@entry=0x7ffc5aea6090, errormsg=errormsg@entry=0x7ffc5aea5938, preview=preview@entry=true) at .../src/nvim/ex_getln.c:2450
#11 0x0000556533a43371 in execute_cmd (eap=0x7ffc5aea6090, cmdinfo=<optimized out>, preview=true) at .../src/nvim/ex_docmd.c:1880
#12 0x0000556533a603dd in cmdpreview_may_show (s=<optimized out>) at .../src/nvim/ex_getln.c:2806
#13 0x0000556533a64f58 in command_line_changed (s=0x7ffc5aea6780) at .../src/nvim/ex_getln.c:2887
#14 0x0000556533a65ac8 in command_line_execute (state=0x7ffc5aea6780, key=<optimized out>) at .../src/nvim/ex_getln.c:1545
#15 0x0000556533c12770 in state_enter (s=0x7ffc5aea6780) at .../src/nvim/state.c:100
```

It doesn't fail with empty `inccomand`.

### Steps to reproduce

- `nvim --clean`
- type `:s/x/y/22222222222222`

### Expected behavior

Something less destructive than crash.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

v0.13.0-dev-270+g32e249dfa6

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

No `inccomand` there if I checked correctly

### Operating system/version

Debian Sid

### Terminal name/version

alacritty

### $TERM environment variable

alacritty

### Installation

from repo
title: Assertion `number <= INT_MAX` fails on inccomand + `:s` with too big `count`
